LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER GENERAL DYNAMICS BROADBAND, INC'S PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. AN ARBITRATOR MUST ISSUE AN AWARD THAT ANSWERS "ALL THE QUESTIONS SUBMITTED TO THE ARBITRATORS THE DECISION OF WHICH IS NECESSARY IN ORDER TO DETERMINE THE CONTROVERSY." ( 1283.4.) IT IS FOR THE ARBITRATOR TO DETERMINE WHICH ISSUES ARE ACTUALLY "NECESSARY" TO THE ULTIMATE DECISION. (MORRIS V. ZUCKERMAN (1968) 69 CAL.2D 686, 690.) HERE, RESPONDENT INFRASTRUCTURE NETWORKS HAD A $3.9 MILLION CROSS-CLAIM. THE ARBITRATOR FOUND IT UNNECESSARY TO DECIDE RESPONDENT'S CROSS-CLAIM IN LIGHT OF HIS AWARD FOR PETITIONER. PETITIONER'S AWARD APPARENTLY MOOTED RESPONDENT'S CLAIMS. THE "[F]INDINGS OF AN ARBITRATOR ON QUESTIONS OF LAW AS WELL AS QUESTIONS OF FACT ARE FINAL AND CONCLUSIVE. IT IS NOT APPROPRIATE FOR COURTS TO . . . TO PASS UPON THE VALIDITY OF THE ARBITRATOR'S REASONING . . . ." (CITY OF OAKLAND V. UNITED PUBLIC EMPLOYEES (1986) 179 CAL.APP.3D 356, 363.). ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jan-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kenneth Keller and Tracy Clements of Keller, Sloan, Roman & Holland, LLP telephone number 415-249-8335 for Petitioner General Dynamics Braodband.
Arjun Sivakumar of Brown Rudnick LLP telephone number 949-440-0226 for Respondent Infrastructure Networks LLC.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PETITIONER GENERAL DYNAMICS BROADBAND, INC'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is granted. An arbitrator must issue an award that answers "all the questions submitted to the arbitrators the decision of which is necessary in order to determine the controversy." ( 1283.4.) It is for the arbitrator to determine which issues are actually "necessary" to the ultimate decision. (Morris v. Zuckerman (1968) 69 Cal.2d 686, 690.) Here, Respondent Infrastructure Networks had a $3.9 million cross-claim. The arbitrator found it unnecessary to decide Respondent's cross-claim in light of his award for Petitioner. Petitioner's award apparently mooted Respondent's claims. The "[f]indings of an arbitrator on questions of law as well as questions of fact are final and conclusive. It is not appropriate for courts to . . . to pass upon the validity of the arbitrator's reasoning . . . ." (City of Oakland v. United Public Employees (1986) 179 Cal.App.3d 356, 363.). Order and judgment signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Dec-14-2015 Continued To Jan-13-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tracy Clements of Keler Sloan Roman Holland LLP telephone number 415-249-8330 for Petitioner General Dynamics Broadband.
Arjun Sivakumar of Brown Rudnick LLP telephone number 949-440-0226 for Respondent Infrastructure Networks, LLC.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER GENERAL DYNAMICS BROADBAND, INC'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is continued to January 13, 2016 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302. Respondent has shown good cause for its requested continuance and petitioner has not shown that it will be prejudiced by the continuance. The Parties agreed to the following briefing schedule: Opposition is due by December 23, 2015. Reply is due by January 6, 2016.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/HK)
